California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
September 9′  ■990
Dennis A. Bar■ow
Office of the City Attorney
city Ha■■
300 North ':D:I Street
San Bernardino′  CA  924■8
Re:  Your Request for lnfOrma■
 AssiStance
Our File No. I-90-532
Dear Mr. Barlow:
You have written seekingT advice on behalf of san Bernardino
city Councilmember Norine Milter regarding the conflict-of-
intlrest provisions of the Potiticai neform Act (the 'Actu).1
Because your request is a general inquiry, we treat your request
as one for informal assj-stance pursuant to Regrulation 18329(c)
(copy enclosed).2
OUESTIONS
1. Ilay council-nember MiLler purchase tickets for travel on
city business from a travel agency oerned by her sister without
creating a conflict of interest under Section 87101?
2. rs a nonprofit private hospital a disqualifyingT source of
incorne to the councilmernber when the councilmember holds a bond
issued by the hospital worth $1o,ooo which yields 5650 in interest
annual ly?
1 Gor".rr."nt Code sections 81OOO-91015. A1l- statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imrnunity provided by an opinion or fornal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Strcet,Suitc 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804‐ 0807 0 (916)322´ 5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-532
Page 2
3. May the councilmember serve as a member of the board of
directors of a nonprofit private hospital without creating a
conflict of interest if the only conpensation is four lunches
provided annually?
CONCLUSTONS
1. The council,member may purchase tickets for city business
fron her sister's travel agency.
2. Interest from a bond issued by a private nonprofit
hospital constitutes a potentially disqualifying financial inter-
est.
3. The councilmember nay serve on the hospital' board of
directors lrithout creating a conflict of interest if her compensa-
tion is less than $250 in the twelve months preceding the deci-
sion.
FACTS
1. councilrnernber uiller 
j.ntends to purchase tickets for
travel on city business from a travel agency owned by her sister.
Presumably, the tj-ckets vril1 be paid for with city funds or
councilmember Miller will seek rej-mbursement from the city.
2. Councilmember Milter holds a $1O,ooo bond from a private
nonprofi,t hospital. This bond yields $650 in interest annually.
The hospital may corne before the city councj,l on Datters such as
zoning variances and general plan amendments.
3. Councilmernber Miller wishes to serve on the Board of
Directors of the same private, nonprofit hospital. 
There is no
compensation provided except four free lunches annual1y.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official 
fron naking,
participatinlr, or in any way attenpting to use his official 
posi-
tion to influence a governnental decision in which he knolrs or has
reason to know he has a financial interest. 
An official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable frorn its effect on the public generally, on the
official, 
a menber of his or her inunediate fanily, or any source
of income over $250 in the preceding 12 months. (Section 87103.)
1. TraveI Tickets: By purchasing travel tickets,
Councilmember Miller is naking a governmental decision because she
is obligating the city to a course of action and entering into a
contractual agreement on behalf of the city. 
(Regulation
18700(b) (3), 18700(b) (4), copy enclosed.) Holrever, the decision
does not involve any financial interest of Councilnenber Mi11er,
her inrnediate family, or a source of incorne to her. 
r'Immediate


Our Fi■e No. I-90-532
Page 3
farnily" means spouse and dependent children (Sectj'on 82029), and
thereiore excludes her sister. 
since the councilnember has no
financial interest in the decisj.on, no conflict of interest arises
under the Act if she purchases travel tickets from the travel
agency o$rned by her sister. r
2. Hospital Bond: The hospital is a source of incorne to
Councilmernber Miller because it pays $650 in interest to her annu-
alIy.a 
(Section 82030(a).) Councilmember uiller must therefore
disgualify herself if the hospital is directly involved in a deci-
sion before the city council. 
(Regulation La702.1(a) (1).)
trDirectly involvedrr generally means the hospital appears before
the city council $rith some sort of request. (Regulation
La702.1(b), copy encLosed. )
If the hospital is indirectly involved in the decision,
trurateriatityt' is defi.ned by Regulation 18702.5 (copy enclosed).
You must delermine the nonprofit entity's gross annual receipts
and the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of the decision to
determine whether councilrnember wiII be required to disqualify
herself.
3. Hospital Board of Directors: As a nonprofit entity, the
hospital is not a "business entity" for purposes of Section.87103 '
lSettion 82005, copy enclosed) ' 
Because the hospital pays its
nembers of the board of directors less than $250 annua11y, the
hospital would not be a source of incorne to Councifrnenber l'!i1Ier
for purposes of the Act. 
(Section 87103(c).) Her membership on
ttre toard would not, in itself, 
create a conflict of interest
because the hospital. is nonprofit. 
(Section 87103(d).)
Therefore, neither the nernbership on the board nor the receipt of
Iunches worth less than $250 would create a conflict of interest
under the Act shoul,d the city councj.l be presented with a decision
regarding the hospital.
' 
The cornmission provides advise concerning the Political 
Reform
Act only. 
You may wish to consult with the Attorney General
regarding other provisions that may app1y, such as Government code
Section 1090.
4 Yo, stated that the hospital is private and that there is a
very lirnited market for the bond. If the hospital were a
governmental agency, the interest vould not constitute a source of
income because interest fron bonds issued by governmental agencies
is excluded fron the definition of incorne. (Section 82030(b)(4).)
In addition, interest on a securi.ty registered with the Securities
and Exchange Cornmj.ssion is excluded fron the definition of incone.
(Section 82030(b) (5).) 
since the bond has a linited resale
narket, I an presuming it is not listed with the Securities and
Exchange Cornmission and thus does not corne within the exception in
Section 82030 (b) (s).


In summary, the purchase of travel tickets from her sister
lrould not create a conflict of interest for councilmenber Mi11er,
nor would membership on the hospital Board of Directors. 
However,
the interest from the hospital bond does estab].ish the hospital as
a source of income to the councilmember and uay result in a
conflict of interest.
I trust this letter adequately addresses your questions. If
you have any further questions regarding thj.s natter, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-59oL.
Our File No. I-90-532
Page 4
SH: PB : aa
Enclosures
s incere Iy,
Scott Hallabrin
Acling ceneral counsel
By: PeggTy Bernard)
counsel, Legal Division
